title: psephellus caucasicus
intro: Psephellus caucasicus, also known as Caucasian psephellus or Caucasus psephellus, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the Caucasus region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Psephellus caucasicus has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. The plant's herbal preparations typically include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
